title: Split login page into separate Admin and Customer entry points

requirements.summary: Create a login selection landing page with clearly labeled Admin and Customer entry points
acceptanceCriteria: A login selection page is shown as the default landing route
Two distinct buttons or sections are visible: one for Admin and one for Customer
Clicking 'Admin Login' navigates to the admin authentication flow
Clicking 'Customer Login' navigates to the customer portal login form

file_operations.description: Create a landing page component that displays two prominent navigation options: 'Admin Login' and 'Customer Login'. Each option should be a card or button that clearly indicates its purpose. Use the authorization component to support role-based navigation. Style consistently with the existing AO Farms theme using Tailwind classes and OKLCH color tokens. Include the AO Farms logo from frontend/public/assets/generated/ao-farms-logo.dim_400x400.png at the top of the page.

Add a new route for the LoginSelection page at the root path '/'. Update the existing routing configuration to include this new landing page as the default entry point. Ensure the route is publicly accessible without authentication.

requirements.summary: Restore working admin login flow using Internet Identity with backend admin verification and dashboard redirect
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Login page shows an Internet Identity login button
After authentication, the backend isAdmin check is called
Successful admin authentication redirects to the admin dashboard
Non-admin principals see a clear 'Access denied' or 'Not authorized' message
Admin session persists across page refreshes

file_operations.description: Create a dedicated admin login page that uses Internet Identity authentication via the useInternetIdentity hook. After successful II authentication, call the backend isCallerAdmin method to verify admin role. On success, redirect to '/admin' (the admin panel). If the principal is not an admin, display a clear error message like 'Access denied: Admin privileges required' and provide a logout button. Use the authorization component to handle authentication state. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add a route for the AdminLogin page at '/admin-login'. Wire this route into the router configuration so it can be accessed from the LoginSelection page.

Update the AdminPanel component to redirect unauthenticated users to '/admin-login' instead of handling login inline. Remove any embedded login UI and rely on the dedicated AdminLogin page. Ensure the component still verifies admin status via isCallerAdmin and shows appropriate loading states.

requirements.summary: Create dedicated Customer Login page with username/password authentication and customer portal redirect
acceptanceCriteria: Customer Login page displays username and password input fields
Submitting valid credentials authenticates the customer via the backend loginCustomer call
Successful login stores the customer session (via CustomerAuthContext) and redirects to the customer portal
Invalid credentials display a user-friendly error message
Customer session persists in sessionStorage across page refreshes

file_operations.description: Create a dedicated customer login page with username and password input fields. Use the useCustomerLogin mutation hook from frontend/src/hooks/useCustomerQueries.ts to authenticate via the backend customerLogin method. On successful authentication, call the CustomerAuthContext login function to store the session in sessionStorage, then redirect to '/portal'. Display user-friendly error messages for invalid credentials, account not found, or inactive accounts. Use shadcn/ui form components (Input, Button, Label) and maintain consistency with the AO Farms theme.
Add a route for the CustomerLogin page at '/customer-login'. Wire this route into the router configuration so it can be accessed from the LoginSelection page.

Update the CustomerPortal component to redirect unauthenticated customers to '/customer-login' instead of showing an inline login form. Remove the embedded login UI and rely on the dedicated CustomerLogin page. Ensure authenticated customers can still access their orders and place new orders using the existing CustomerAuthContext.

requirements.summary: Update app routing to use login selection as root and protect admin and customer portal routes
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to '/' shows the login selection page
Navigating directly to an admin route without authentication redirects to admin login
Navigating directly to the customer portal without a customer session redirects to customer login
Both portals are fully inaccessible without proper authentication

file_operations.description: Update the root route '/' to render the LoginSelection component. Add route guards or wrapper components for '/admin' and child admin routes that check authentication and admin status, redirecting to '/admin-login' if unauthorized. Add route guards for '/portal' that check customer authentication via CustomerAuthContext, redirecting to '/customer-login' if not authenticated. Ensure all protected routes properly enforce authentication before rendering content.

Update the Layout component navigation to conditionally show admin and customer portal links based on authentication state. For unauthenticated users, show a link to the login selection page. Ensure the navigation reflects the new routing structure with separate admin and customer entry points.
